description:
Choropleth map displaying the spatial distribution of an unspecified metric across Europe, delineated by sub-national administrative regions (likely NUTS regions), with country borders outlined in red. The legend categorises the metric values into six ranges, represented by shades of green, and includes a "No Data" category in pink.

The value ranges are:
*   Pink: No Data
*   Lightest green: 0 - 2
*   Lighter green: 2 - 3.4
*   Medium green: 3.4 - 5
*   Darker green: 5 - 7.6
*   Darkest green: 7.6 - 12.2

Spatially, the highest values (7.6 - 12.2, darkest green) are concentrated in Ireland, parts of Scotland, specific regions in coastal France, and northern areas of Scandinavia (Norway, Sweden, Finland). Medium-high values (5 - 7.6) are also present in Sweden and Finland. Central European countries, including Germany, Czechia, Poland, Austria, and Hungary, predominantly show lower values (0 - 2 and 2 - 3.4). Southern European countries like Spain, Italy, and Greece, along with Turkey, mostly exhibit lower to medium values (0 - 2 to 3.4 - 5), with some localised exceptions. Most of Iceland's territory is marked as "No Data", with some regions in the 0-2 range. No scale bar, compass, specific data source, or reference year is provided in the image.
